body: PT Sarana Multigriya Finansial (no ticker) plans to issue shelf-registration bonds worth IDR 6.2 trillion next month, with IDR 5 trillion allocated for sustainable bonds and IDR 1.2 trillion for sukuk musyarakah, to be used for financing purposes. The bonds will have varying interest rates, with IDR 704.92 billion fully committed and the remaining IDR 4.29 trillion to be offered on a best-effort basis, with several underwriters and guarantors including PT Bahana Sekuritas, PT Cimb Niaga Sekuritas, and PT DBS Vickers Sekuritas Indonesia.
title: PT Sarana Multigriya Finansial to Issue Bonds and Sukuk Worth IDR 6.2 Trillion for Financing Purposes

body: Ridwan Kamil, Independent Commissioner of PT Ingriga Pratama Capitalindo Tbk (GRIA), shared plans to expand Jabarano, a coffee business, by integrating the coffee supply chain and increasing the number of outlets, with a target of IDR 50 billion in capital expenditure this year to open 10-15 new branches. The business aims to scale beyond 10 branches, with potential revenue growth similar to Starbucks' IDR 300 trillion revenue from its global network outlets.
title: Ridwan Kamil Shares Insights on Expanding Jabarano Coffee Business, Aiming to Unite Supply Chain from Cultivation to Retail.

body: Jonathan Tahir increased his stake in PT Sejahteraaraya Anugrahjaya Tbk (SRAJ) by IDR 31.63 billion, raising his shareholding from 58.25 million to 75.07 million shares, equivalent to a 0.63% stake. This investment increases his portion from 0.49% of the total listed shares, which stood at 12 billion as of December 2024.
title: Jonathan Tahir increases stake in Mayapada Hospital network's managing company PT Sejahteraraya Anugrahjaya with IDR 31.63 billion investment.

body: PT Wijaya Karya Tbk (WIKA) secured new contracts worth IDR 20.66 trillion in December 2024, a increase from IDR 19.96 trillion in November 2024, with the majority coming from the infrastructure building segment. The company is currently handling 75 construction projects across Indonesia, including 42 national strategic projects, and expects these projects to contribute to its cash management efficiency and support the government's grand vision through the ASTA CITA program.
title: PT Wijaya Karya secures new contracts worth IDR 20.66 trillion in December 2024, driven by infrastructure building and industrial construction support.

body: SMKL (SMKL) is establishing a joint venture, PT Satyamitra Yisong Indonesia, with Guangzhou Yi Song Paper Products Company Ltd, which is expected to positively impact the company's growth through paper pulp moulding product development. In September 2024, SMKL recorded revenue of IDR 1.30 trillion, with a comprehensive profit of IDR 23.31 billion, a significant increase from IDR 12.31 billion.
title: SMKL Establishes Joint Venture with Guangzhou Yi Song Paper Products to Develop Paper Pulp Moulding Products.

body: PT Ina Sekuritas Indonesia projects crude palm oil (CPO) prices to remain high due to weather challenges in Malaysia and Indonesia, raising its price projection for FY25/26F by 20-30% to MYR 4,700-4,800 per ton. The firm recommends an "overweight" rating for the plantation sector, with "buy" recommendations for shares of PT Perusahaan Perkebunan London Sumatra Indonesia Tbk (LSIP) and PT Astra Agro Lestari Tbk (AALI), citing potential harvest declines and historical La Nina effects.
title: Weather challenges in Malaysia and Indonesia may maintain high crude palm oil prices and impact production.

body: PT Wijaya Karya Gedung Tbk (WIKA) secured new contracts worth IDR 2.66 trillion in 2024, driven by strategic projects across various sectors, including offices, public facilities, and residential projects. The company aims to reach a new contract target of IDR 3.58 trillion in 2025, a 34.43% increase from last year, with the majority of contracts expected to come from the government, state-owned enterprises, and the private sector.
title: Wika Gedung Secures New Contracts Worth IDR 2.66 Trillion in 2024 Across Various Sectors.

body: PT Perma Plasindo Tbk (no ticker provided) saw a 34.35% increase to IDR 176 per share, while PT Remala Abadi Tbk (no ticker provided) and PT Saraswanti Anugerah Makmur Tbk (no ticker provided) followed with 24.90% and 24.85% increases, respectively. On the other hand, PT Kencana Energi Lestari Tbk (no ticker provided) experienced a 16.58% decrease, while PT Trust Finance Indonesia Tbk (no ticker provided) and PT Pudjiadi & Sons Tbk (no ticker provided) saw 12.56% and 9.45% drops, respectively.
title: Indonesian Stock Market Sees Mixed Performance with Gainers and Losers.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) saw a positive shift despite a net sell trend, with a total transaction value of IDR 12.66 trillion and trading volume of 18.85 billion shares, led by the infrastructure sector's 1.05% growth. Foreign investors recorded a net sell of IDR 383.24 billion, with PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) topping the transaction value at IDR 1.5 trillion, and the JCI has seen a 1.44% year-to-date increase.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Rises Despite Net Sell Trend and Foreign Investor Caution

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1.71 billion shares on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, with Goto shares from PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (TKPK) experiencing the largest net sell of 1.44 billion shares. Meanwhile, PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) topped the buy list with a net purchase of 58 million shares, followed by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with a net buy of 38.68 million shares.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Sees Net Sell of 1.71 Billion Shares by Foreign Investors

body: PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk is repaying its 2020 bonds worth IDR 750 billion, which will mature on February 12, 2025, with the principal bond payment and final coupon to be deposited with PT Kustodian Sentral Efek Indonesia. As of September 2024, the company had cash and cash equivalents of USD 1.20 billion, equity of USD 2.90 billion, and long-term and short-term bond liabilities of USD 605.53 million and USD 107.97 million, respectively.
title: Chandra Asri Pacific to Repay 2020 Bonds Worth IDR 750 Billion Ahead of Maturity Date.

body: PT Ingria Pratama Capitalindo Tbk (INGF) has set a sales target of 642 housing units for 2025, doubling its actual sales of 330 units in the previous year, with 418 units already available as ready-to-occupy stock since January 2025. The company is optimistic about achieving its revenue target of IDR 120.8 billion and net profit of IDR 7.1 billion in 2025, driven by its plan to build 693 units and sell 642 units this year.
title: PT Ingria Pratama Capitalindo sets ambitious sales target of 642 housing units for 2025, doubling previous year's sales.

body: Indonesia's cement industry faces challenges from fierce competition and changes in government infrastructure policies, which may lead to a decline in bulk cement demand, but bagged cement sales, which account for 70% of total national consumption, are expected to offset the decline. PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk (INTP) is projected to outperform PT Semen Indonesia Tbk (SMGR) in terms of top-line performance, with a 10% increase in sales volume in the first seven months of 2024, driven by ongoing market expansion.
title: Indonesian Cement Industry Faces Challenges Amidst Infrastructure Budget Cuts, But Bagged Cement Sales Offer Promise

body: PT Remala Abadi TBK (TOWR) saw a 59.09% surge in its shares, increasing by 445 points to IDR 1,225 per share, resulting in a IDR 1.58 trillion rise. The company is acquiring a 40% stake in PT Iforte Solusi Infotek through its subsidiary PT Sarana Menara Nusantara TBK, with the aim of expanding its business and strengthening its digital telecommunication infrastructure.
title: PT Remala Abadi's shares surge 59% after announcing acquisition of subsidiary PT Sarana Menara Nusantara's data portion.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index rose 0.22% to 7,170.73 on Monday, driven by gains in banking stocks such as PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BBNI), which contributed a combined 36.57 points to the index. Meanwhile, stocks with large market capitalization, including PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), PT Barito Renewables Energi Tbk, and PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM), dragged the index down, with foreign investors recording a net sell of IDR 276.18 billion.
title: Jakarta Composite Index rises 0.22% to 7,170.73 on Monday, led by banking stocks.
